summary.intent: Implement a native ReadSkillTool for the moltis gateway that allows agents to read skill files (SKILL.md and sidecars) without leaking absolute paths, as specified in a pre-written plan. Then address PR review comments iteratively until Greptile reached 5/5 confidence.
summary.outcome: PR #630 was created and pushed to github.com/moltis-org/moltis with 4 commits implementing ReadSkillTool, a safety/injection-scanner module, updated prompt_gen, gateway registration, and integration tests. Five beads issues were closed; Greptile iterated through 4 review cycles ending at 4/5 with a final iteration 4 commit pushed.

repo: Skills are discovered via FsSkillDiscoverer; gateway server.rs registers skill tools in a dedicated block and must use data_dir-scoped discoverer (not default_paths()) to stay consistent with other skill tools.
normalize_relative_skill_file_path in parse.rs is the canonical containment guard reused across Write/Delete/Read tools — new tools must call it for all file path inputs.
The hermes-agent skill system (Python) has a skills_tool.py with skill_view, skills_list, and skills_categories; the assets/ sidecar directory convention comes from agentskills.io standard.
moltis-csq is a pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure that blocks `just lint` — use targeted per-crate clippy as the equivalent coverage.
Beads issues are claimed/closed via CLI bash commands; the session-close protocol treats worktree branches as ephemeral and does not push Dolt remotes.
The greploop skill drives iterative PR-comment resolution: trigger Greptile, poll check status by SHA, fetch inline comments, fix, repeat until 5/5 or zero unresolved threads.

Greptile reviews flag: blocking sync I/O (is_dir) inside async fns, missing symlink-rejection guards on all read paths, missing size caps on plugin read branches, and per-subdirectory sidecar listing caps to avoid silent truncation of whole directories.

code.finding: ReadSkillTool must apply the 128KB cap on BOTH directory-backed and plugin read paths; early implementations only capped the directory path, leaving the plugin branch unbounded.
collect_sidecar_entries must use async fs calls (tokio::fs::metadata) instead of blocking std::fs::metadata/is_dir() because it runs inside an async context.
Symlink-escape rejection (lstat check) must be applied to every read entry point: read_primary, read_sidecar, AND collect_sidecar_entries — missing it on any one branch is a security gap.
A shared 16-file cap across all sidecar subdirs can silently drop entire subdirectories; fix is a per-subdir quota (e.g., 8 per subdir) so no single subdir can exhaust the budget.
A tolerant strip_optional_frontmatter helper was added to avoid crashing on plugin-style skill content that includes YAML frontmatter before the body.
prompt_gen must mention assets/ sidecar directory in the activation instruction, and path-leak tests should use individual assertions (not ||) so failures are specific.
ReadSkillTool was initially registered with default_paths() while all other skill tools use data_dir — must use the data_dir-scoped FsSkillDiscoverer for consistency.

workflow: Run targeted per-crate clippy (moltis-skills, moltis-tools, moltis-agents, moltis-gateway) instead of `just lint` when a pre-existing unrelated build failure blocks the full lint recipe.
The greploop skill automates the fix→compile→test→commit→push→trigger→poll→fetch→fix cycle for PR review comments; invoke it after initial comment fetch.
Format check (cargo fmt --check) produces empty output on success — empty stdout is the pass signal, not a non-zero exit.
Integration tests in crates/gateway/tests/ are the right place to assert that a tool is actually registered in the ToolRegistry, catching wiring bugs that unit tests miss.

friction: just lint blocked by pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure (moltis-csq) — had to fall back to per-crate clippy invocations.
bd dolt pull failed in the worktree — no Dolt remote configured, so the pull-beads-updates step was a no-op.
Greptile required 4 full review iterations (trigger → poll → fix → push → repeat) to reach clean; each iteration introduced new P2 findings not visible in earlier rounds.
Background task notification summaries were occasionally misleading — `just lint` task was labeled exit code 0 in the summary but actually exited 101 due to the CUDA build failure.
Resolving Greptile inline review threads required GraphQL API calls (gh api graphql) rather than a simpler gh pr command.
open_items: moltis-avv (token-budget fallback for skill prompts) — explicitly left open as specified in the original plan.
skills_list and skills_categories progressive-disclosure tools (hermes-agent port) — deferred, maps to moltis-avv scope.
Platform gating in skill frontmatter (e.g., only activate on macOS) — needs frontmatter spec change, not implemented.
Required env var declarations in skill frontmatter — not implemented.
Slashy path resolution for skill names — not implemented.
Greptile ended iteration 4 at 4/5 (not 5/5) — the final commit addressing per-subdir sidecar cap and data_dir consistency was pushed but not yet re-reviewed.
